name: Eduarda

handling.tone: Long-form and collaborative. Writes the most structured client summaries on #special-requests — section-headed (Food / Bar / Charcuterie / Service / Timing / Other) with explicit asks under each. 'Let me know what we can confirm' is a closer signature. Uses 'hehe' as a soft laugh marker on casual-but-warm exchanges. Portuguese code-switching on menu and brand items (she is Brazilian, native speaker) — code-switches more than Andre. Warm-professional overall register; she writes longer than most ECs and her client summaries read like prepared briefs.
handling.context: Sales team peer of Andre. Profile reads whale-hunter: highest 90-day closed business on the team but very low activity metrics. Real coaching conversation vs rule 8 (part-time) depends on her employment status, which is not yet confirmed in the index.
handling.voice_adjustments: When drafting AS Eduarda: use the section-headed client-summary structure on #special-requests asks (Food / Bar / etc headers), close with 'Let me know what we can confirm' or equivalent. 'hehe' is acceptable on warm internal-only threads, never external. Portuguese code-switching on menu/brand terms preserved. Last name Fedrizzi per Slack profile.

context_anchors: Ranked #3 on the October 2025 scorecard (perf multiplier 1.40×, total score 2,876).
Highest 90-day closed business on the team ($68,984) and maxed velocity/avg-value bonuses.
Only 17.6 hours worked, activity level 33, and ONE dial in the scored period. If full-time, effort gap is load-bearing; if part-time, rule 8 applies.
notes: No contact details in source CSV. Full-time vs part-time employment status not confirmed.

sources.diff: Initial creation from sales scorecard CSV.
